nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that compiles the Go backend and Vite frontend, then assembles a Node.js runtime image bundling llama-lab, Ollama, and supervisor, exposing port 5866.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-stage build: golang builder for the backend binary, node builder for the frontend dist, and a slim node runtime that installs Ollama and runs under supervisord.

nodes.name: backend-builder
nodes.summary: Golang build stage that downloads modules and compiles the backend into a static /app/bin/llama-lab binary from ./cmd/main.go.

nodes.name: frontend-builder
nodes.summary: Node build stage that installs npm dependencies and produces the production frontend bundle via npm run build.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Compose file that builds and runs the single llama-lab service, wiring up named volumes for config/private/logs/state plus a host-mounted Ollama models directory and a rich set of environment variables.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ${VAR:-default} interpolation for all env vars and mounts persistent named volumes so container state survives restarts.

nodes.name: llama-lab
nodes.summary: The llama-lab container service: builds from the local Dockerfile, publishes port 5866, sets custom DNS, and persists config, private secrets, logs, state, and Ollama models via volumes.
